behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at the boundary between manifest validation and module loading, not just manifest schema logic. REAL-INFRA: only local filesystem fixtures are needed (not System). MOCK-HIDE: mocking import/execution can miss accidental eager loads; real fixture plugins with side effects catch this. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one invalid plugin (manifest missing a required field) whose entry module has a visible side effect (e.g., writes a marker file or mutates shared state at top-level). Run the real loader against the directory and assert: (1) loader reports manifest validation failure for that plugin, and (2) the side effect never occurs.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's onLoad hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior depends on interaction between dependency-resolution and lifecycle invocation modules. Unit-testing topological sort alone is insufficient. REAL-INFRA: local fixtures only. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing loader internals can hide wiring/order regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Set up temp plugins A and B with valid manifests where A depends on B. Each onLoad appends its name to a shared in-memory log or temp file. Execute the real loader and assert load succeeds and observed hook order is ["B", "A"].

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: cycle detection must be validated together with execution gating (neither plugin loads). REAL-INFRA: local plugin manifests/files only. MOCK-HIDE: mocking graph results can miss a bug where cycle is detected but plugins still execute.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with mutual dependencies and entry modules that produce unmistakable side effects if executed. Run the real loader and assert: (1) a cycle error/report mentions A<->B, and (2) neither plugin's entry/onLoad side effects occur.

behaviors.description: A plugin's onLoad hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is an ordering contract at the module-loader boundary (module execution vs hook invocation). Unit tests of hook dispatcher alone miss it. REAL-INFRA: no external network/process needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocked import can falsely preserve expected order.
behaviors.test_description: Build a plugin whose entry module sets a top-level flag/value during import, and whose onLoad asserts that flag is already set (or records timestamp/order). Run loader with the real import path and assert onLoad runs only after entry-module side effect is present.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its onLoad hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires dependency graph handling + per-plugin error isolation + continuation logic across multiple plugins. REAL-INFRA: local fixtures only. MOCK-HIDE: mocking hook executor may miss real short-circuit/abort behavior in orchestrator.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: F (throws in onLoad), I (independent), and D (depends on F). Run loader and assert: F failure is reported, I onLoad still runs successfully, and D is skipped/not initialized due to dependency on failed F.
